story: “Pour me a glass of poison!” he used to call as he came through the door. Tonight I sit across from him, past fear. I stirred a tasteless powder into his favorite whiskey and set the decanter back on the cart. He comes in loosening his tie, asks why I skipped my shift; I lie about a migraine and prescriptions. He scans my face, then the room. He checks his phone, drops onto the couch opposite, reaches for the waiting tumbler, and lifts it up.
implausible_knowledge: I stirred a tasteless powder into his favorite whiskey and set the decanter back on the cart.
question: What is he most likely to do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Set the glass aside without drinking.
